name: Focaccia (Italian Flatbread) ABM
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, dried rosemary, salt, and yeast.
2. Add the olive oil and hot water, mixing on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the sides of the bowl constantly.
3. Continue mixing on medium speed for 1 minute, scraping the bowl frequently.
4. Stir in enough remaining flour to make the dough easy to handle. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface.
5. Knead the dough for 5 to 8 minutes or until smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size (the dough should be ready if an indentation remains when touched).
6. Punch down the dough. Brush a 12-inch pizza pan or large cookie sheet with olive oil.
7. Press the dough into the pan or flatten it into a 12-inch circle on the cookie sheet. Make indentations, about 2 inches apart, with your fingers.
8. Brush the indentations with oil and sprinkle with pepper. Let rise uncovered in a warm place for an additional 30 minutes.
9. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown, brushing with additional oil if desired. Serve warm.
If using quick-acting yeast, omit the first rise. After kneading, cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes before pressing into the pan and continuing as directed. High altitude directions (3500 to 6500 feet): Rising times may be slightly shorter.
